sections.paragraph:
an unused root.-I. Arab. أَحْأَحَ onomatop. from the sound אָח, to cry out ah! repeatedly. In Hebrew perhaps, to groan (نchzen), to howl, whence אֹחִים. The Arabs have under the same root-

II. the signification of heat, burning, anger, in the word أَحَاحُ, أُحَاحُ, whence, perhaps, may be derived אָח, إِخُّ a pot, a furnace. I had rather however take the signification of furnace, from the root أَجَّ to burn, to kindle as fire; Conj. II. to set on fire, أَجَّةُ heat, etc. See .
